entity.summary: Arbitrum-native decentralized exchange operating V2 and V3 liquidity venues, routing, farming, staking, limit-order, and TWAP functions under the Arbidex and Arbitrum Exchange names.
entity.notes: HEI normalizes the launch date to 2023-01-01 because available security and market references establish a 2023 operating start but do not preserve an exact launch day. The official application and documentation remain reachable, but the application displayed zero headline TVL and volume during review and CoinGecko reported no current ARX trading pairs, so status is limited rather than fully active. Arbitrum Exchange V2 and V3 are versions of this protocol-level entity.

events.title: Arbidex entered public operation in 2023
events.description: Arbidex began operating on Arbitrum during 2023 with its ARX token and decentralized exchange functions.
events.notes: The exact launch day was not recovered. The date is normalized to the start of the independently documented 2023 launch year; market history confirms operation by March 2023.

events.title: Arbidex integrated Orbs dLIMIT and dTWAP
events.description: Arbidex added decentralized limit-order and time-weighted execution functions powered by the Orbs network.
events.notes: The dated Orbs regional publication and Orbs primary article document the same integration.

evidence.title: ArbiDex Token Smart Contract Audit
evidence.notes: Contract profile identifies ARX as an Arbitrum token launched in 2023 and links it to arbidex.fi.

evidence.title: Arbidex price and market history
evidence.notes: ARX market history records a March 26, 2023 all-time high, independently establishing public operation by March 2023.

evidence.title: Arbidex integrates dLIMIT and dTWAP powered by Orbs
evidence.notes: Technology provider announcement documents the integration, Arbidex trading functions, and contemporaneous transaction volume.

evidence.title: Arbidex integrates Orbs dLIMIT and dTWAP
evidence.notes: Dated regional Orbs publication corroborates the integration date and product scope.

evidence.title: Arbidex introduction
evidence.notes: First-party documentation defines the Arbidex protocol, V2/V3 functions, routing, farming, staking, and Arbitrum identity.

evidence.title: Arbidex application
evidence.notes: The official interface remains reachable and exposes V2, V3, staking, bridge, and perpetual links, but displayed zero headline TVL and volume during review.
